title: Liquid laundry detergent
subtitle: A concentrated washing soda and castile soap liquid detergent for machine and hand washing
excerpt: Washing soda raises the wash water pH to help lift grease and organic soils; diluted castile soap adds surfactant cleaning power. This small-batch concentrated detergent uses 50 ml per standard machine load. It suits most fabrics at 30 to 40°C and rinses cleanly. Lavender essential oil is optional fragrance. Yield is approximately 240 ml.
sourceNotes: Washing soda (sodium carbonate, Na2CO3) as a laundry booster: raises wash water pH to 11, which increases the effectiveness of surfactants. Not the same as bicarbonate of soda (sodium bicarbonate, NaOH baking soda). Castile soap in laundry: effective as a surfactant in soft water areas; can leave a film in hard water areas (mineral deposits react with soap). Adding citric acid to the rinse cycle reduces hard water soap film. Usage rate: 50 ml per standard load (7 kg). Essential oil in laundry: fragrance is present at low concentration and largely rinses out; any scent on clothing will be subtle.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 240 ml concentrated detergent (approximately 4 to 5 standard machine loads)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. Store in a labelled bottle. Shake before each use.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. The mixture may thicken slightly on standing; shake to redistribute.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 6 months in a sealed bottle. Shake before use as ingredients may separate slightly. Discard if the detergent develops mould or an off smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: Sodium carbonate (Na2CO3), also called soda ash. Not the same as bicarbonate of soda (sodium bicarbonate, NaHCO3). Washing soda raises the pH of wash water significantly higher, which increases cleaning power and helps activate surfactants. Handle with protective gloves as it is alkaline and can irritate skin.

content.text: as the alkalinity builder and diluted castile soap as the surfactant. Together they clean effectively at 30 to 40°C on most fabric types. The one caveat is hard water: soap reacts with calcium and magnesium ions in hard water to form a scummy film. Adding a small amount of citric acid or white vinegar to the machine's fabric softener compartment during the rinse cycle neutralises this and removes soap film. In soft water areas, this detergent rinses cleanly without that step. Use protective gloves when handling the washing soda before it is dissolved.

items.prepNote: Warm slightly to help dissolve the washing soda
Sodium carbonate; wear protective gloves while dissolving
Fragrance; adds a subtle scent that mostly rinses out

content.text: Wear protective gloves. Gently warm the distilled water to about 50°C; do not boil. Pour into a jug and add the washing soda. Stir until fully dissolved. Allow to cool to room temperature.

Add the castile soap to the cooled washing soda solution. Stir gently; do not whisk or shake as this creates foam. Add the lavender essential oil and stir to combine. Pour into the bottle using the funnel.

Shake gently before use. Add 50 ml to the machine's detergent drawer for a standard 7 kg load. For hand washing, add 15 to 20 ml to a basin of warm water. For hard water areas, add 30 ml of white vinegar to the fabric softener compartment during the rinse cycle to remove soap film.
Store in the sealed bottle at room temperature. Shake before each use. Keeps 6 months. Discard if mould appears or if the mixture smells off.
